counterargument: A single global target can penalize hard tasks and inflate easy-task confidence
boundary_condition_not_hold: Mixed task portfolio with different difficulty distributions
recommendation_for_c: Define tiered targets by task class and language

counterargument: Judge-model variance and rubric drift can make fixed threshold unreliable
boundary_condition_not_hold: No calibrated judge agreement or periodic rubric audits
recommendation_for_c: Add inter-rater agreement and calibration KPI

claim_text: tool_call_accuracy >=0.95 is sufficient for safe operation
counterargument: High aggregate accuracy can hide catastrophic failure on high-risk tools
boundary_condition_not_hold: Toolset includes write/exec operations with asymmetric risk

counterargument: Error-centric trigger misses semantic failures and silent degradations
recommendation_for_c: Add semantic and policy breach rollback triggers

claim_text: cost_per_success >1.25 baseline should trigger model routing
counterargument: Cost-only routing can degrade quality if quality constraints are weak
boundary_condition_not_hold: No quality floor in cost optimization policy
recommendation_for_c: Require quality floor before any cost-driven reroute

claim_text: trace_id coverage >=99% is enough for incident debugging
counterargument: Coverage metric can be high while critical spans are missing
boundary_condition_not_hold: Trace exists but key release_unit fields absent

claim_text: golden_core 120 samples makes PR gate meaningful
counterargument: Sample count alone does not ensure representativeness
boundary_condition_not_hold: Domain skew and stale examples dominate dataset
recommendation_for_c: Add freshness/diversity and business-critical coverage checks

claim_text: adversarial_set 80 samples is enough safety baseline
counterargument: Attack evolution can outpace fixed small corpus
recommendation_for_c: Add rolling adversarial refresh SLA and coverage matrix

claim_text: rollback within 10 minutes is feasible as a universal SLO
counterargument: Universal rollback SLO ignores approval chains and data migration dependencies
boundary_condition_not_hold: Cross-team controlled releases with manual approvals

counterargument: Zero-success can coexist with large unknown-unknown attack surface
boundary_condition_not_hold: Attack suite lacks multi-step chained abuse scenarios
recommendation_for_c: Pair with adversarial scenario coverage KPI

counterargument: 5% miss on high-risk tools can still be unacceptable for regulated workflows
Incident count is lagging; absence of incidents does not prove resistance
recommendation_for_c: Add proactive sandbox breakout test cadence KPI

counterargument: Detection blind spots can hide leaks despite zero event count
boundary_condition_not_hold: No red-team seeded canaries and sink-side DLP verification
recommendation_for_c: Add canary token leakage test and downstream validation

counterargument: Signed but malicious or vulnerable artifacts can still pass
boundary_condition_not_hold: No policy for vulnerability severity and provenance quality
recommendation_for_c: Combine signature gate with vuln/provenance quality gates

claim_text: Archived projects should only be corpus references
counterargument: Archived projects with no-license metadata can still create legal uncertainty even as corpus
boundary_condition_not_hold: No explicit legal handling for test-data licensing
recommendation_for_c: Add legal review for benchmark/corpus ingestion

claim_text: NOASSERTION projects can be conditionally included with legal review
counterargument: Repeated NOASSERTION in core dependencies increases decision latency and operational uncertainty
boundary_condition_not_hold: Fast release cadence requiring deterministic dependency policy

counterargument: SLA can be met with low-value samples and poor labeling quality

claim_text: 90-day rollout sequence is execution-ready as is
counterargument: Sequence lacks explicit resource contention fallback and parallelization strategy
recommendation_for_c: Add critical path + fallback staffing plan before execution sign-off
